# komodo_builder (Resource)

Manages a Komodo builder resource.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "komodo_builder" "server" {
  name         = "my-builder"
  builder_type = "Server"

  server_config = {
    server_id = komodo_server.example.id
  }
}

resource "komodo_builder" "url" {
  name         = "peripheral-builder"
  builder_type = "Url"

  url_config = {
    address = "https://periphery.example.com"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `builder_type` (String) The builder type. One of `Url`, `Server`, `Aws`. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `name` (String) The unique name of the builder. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Optional

- `aws_config` (Attributes) Configuration for an AWS builder. Required when `builder_type` is `Aws`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--aws_config))
- `server_config` (Attributes) Configuration for a server builder. Required when `builder_type` is `Server`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--server_config))
- `url_config` (Attributes) Configuration for a URL builder. Required when `builder_type` is `Url`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--url_config))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The builder identifier (ObjectId).

<a id="nestedatt--aws_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `aws_config`

Optional:

- `ami_id` (String) The EC2 AMI ID to use. The AMI must have the Periphery client configured to start at boot.
- `assign_public_ip` (Boolean) Whether to assign the instance a public IP address.
- `insecure_tls` (Boolean) Whether to skip TLS certificate validation for the Periphery connection.
- `instance_type` (String) The EC2 instance type to use for the build.
- `key_pair_name` (String) The key pair name to attach to the instance.
- `periphery_public_key` (String) An expected public key associated with the Periphery private key. If empty, the public key is not validated.
- `port` (Number) The port that Periphery is running on. Default: `8120`.
- `region` (String) The AWS region to create the instance in.
- `secrets` (List of String) The secret names available on the AMI.
- `security_group_ids` (List of String) The security group IDs to attach to the instance.
- `subnet_id` (String) The subnet ID to create the instance in.
- `use_https` (Boolean) Whether to use HTTPS to communicate with the Periphery agent.
- `use_public_ip` (Boolean) Whether Komodo core should communicate with the instance using its public IP address.
- `user_data` (String) The user data script to run when the instance starts.
- `volume_gb` (Number) The size of the builder volume in GiB.


<a id="nestedatt--server_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `server_config`

Optional:

- `server_id` (String) The ID of the Komodo server to use as the builder.


<a id="nestedatt--url_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `url_config`

Optional:

- `address` (String) The address of the Periphery agent.
- `insecure_tls` (Boolean) Whether to skip TLS certificate validation for the Periphery connection.
- `passkey` (String) Deprecated. Optional passkey for Periphery authentication.
- `periphery_public_key` (String) An expected public key associated with the Periphery private key. If empty, the public key is not validated.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import komodo_builder.example 6627c3e4f1a2b3c4d5e6f7a8
```
